title: Qwen/DashScope embeddings in the embedding engine (foundation for dense retrieval + Alibaba surface)
current_state: memory/semantic_dedup.py::get_embedding() is Gemini-only: it calls _gemini_client() and client.models.embed_content(model=EMBEDDING_MODEL, contents=text) via google.genai. With no GEMINI_API_KEY it returns None, so BOTH semantic dedup (T-080) and any dense retrieval silently no-op. The hackathon build routes generation through Qwen but embeddings can't run on Alibaba Cloud at all.
target_state: get_embedding() selects a provider: when QWEN_API_KEY is set it uses Alibaba DashScope text-embedding-v3 (OpenAI-compatible /embeddings endpoint, base https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1) first, Gemini as fallback. Return type stays List[float]; every caller (compute_embedding_for_write, memory_search_semantic, cosine_similarity) is unchanged. Embeddings now run on Alibaba Cloud, which is extra proof-of-use for the submission.
migration_plan: 1. Add EMBEDDING_PROVIDER/QWEN embedding constants: reuse QWEN_API_KEY from app/config.py; DashScope embedding model 'text-embedding-v3' (env QWEN_EMBEDDING_MODEL to override).

2. Add a _qwen_embed(text) using the already-installed openai SDK: OpenAI(api_key=QWEN_API_KEY, base_url=<dashscope compatible-mode>).embeddings.create(model=..., input=text) -> resp.data[0].embedding. Never raises; returns None on failure (match get_embedding's contract).
3. In get_embedding(): try Qwen when QWEN_API_KEY present, else Gemini. Keep the empty-string guard and the never-raise behavior.
4. Unit test (mock the openai client): assert _qwen_embed hits the DashScope base_url + text-embedding-v3, and get_embedding returns the mocked vector when QWEN_API_KEY is set and Gemini is absent.
risk_notes: Embedding DIMENSION differs by provider (Gemini gemini-embedding ~768/3072, Qwen text-embedding-v3 = 1024). Vectors stored under one provider must NOT be cosine-compared against another provider's vectors. cosine_similarity already returns 0.0 on dimension mismatch, so a mixed store degrades to 'no match' (safe, not wrong). If Ash ever switches provider on an existing store, a re-embed pass is needed — note this in code, do not auto-migrate here. Keep this ticket tiny: provider selection + one helper + one test, nothing else.
